page: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8i.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Its single-core performance is more moderate, ranking in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core tests, though Geekbench single-core scores remain in the top 10%. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, alongside strong write performance. The CPU's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), enables top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations.

This hardware profile is highly suited for compute-heavy and memory-intensive general-purpose workloads. The inclusion of AMX instruction sets drives top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation, making it viable for CPU-based machine learning tasks without dedicated GPUs. It also excels in database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, as evidenced by top-tier results in Redis SET operations and Geekbench Clang compilation. From a resource density perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced allocation, providing high multi-core throughput and memory bandwidth for enterprise applications, though workloads requiring high local storage IOPS will need to account for the lack of local NVMe drives.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) running at 3.9 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the m8i.32xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Its support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 enables top-tier performance in cryptography, physics simulations, and CPU-based LLM inference. This instance is highly suited for enterprise databases, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded computational workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-count general-purpose instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and AI inference
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for enterprise databases, web serving, and software compilation
